mission.title: Install and Configure Openfeature on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenFeature is an open specification that provides a vendor-agnostic, community-driven API for feature flagging. Installing OpenFeature allows you to integrate feature flag management into your applications seamlessly. The OpenFeature Operator requires cert-manager as a prerequisite.

steps.title: Install cert-manager (required prerequisite)
steps.description:
The OpenFeature Operator requires cert-manager to issue webhook certificates. Install cert-manager if it is not already present on the cluster:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.14.3/cert-manager.yaml
```

Wait for all cert-manager deployments to become Available before continuing:

```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available=True deploy --all -n cert-manager --timeout=300s
```

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenFeature Helm repository to your local Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add openfeature https://open-feature.github.io/open-feature-operator/
```

steps.title: Update the Helm repository
steps.description:
Update your local Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenFeature
steps.description:
Install (or upgrade) the OpenFeature operator using Helm:
```bash
helm upgrade --install openfeature openfeature/open-feature-operator
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the OpenFeature operator pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-feature-operator-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OpenFeature operator using Helm:
```bash
helm uninstall openfeature
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with OpenFeature. The v1beta1 API replaces `FeatureFlagProvider` with `FeatureFlagSource` and `InProcessConfiguration`:
```bash
kubectl delete crd featureflags.core.openfeature.dev
kubectl delete crd featureflagsources.core.openfeature.dev
kubectl delete crd inprocessconfigurations.core.openfeature.dev
```

Note: CRD names/groups should be verified against the installed chart version — see the official installation docs: https://github.com/open-feature/open-feature-operator/blob/main/docs/installation.md

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the operator namespace to clean up any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace open-feature-operator-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration of the OpenFeature operator:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-feature-operator-system -o yaml > openfeature-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is updated to get the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenFeature
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the OpenFeature operator to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade --install openfeature openfeature/open-feature-operator
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the OpenFeature operator pod is running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-feature-operator-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your OpenFeature pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for insufficient resources in your cluster:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n open-feature-operator-system
``` 
Ensure that your cluster has enough CPU and memory resources available.

troubleshooting.title: OpenFeature operator pod crashes
troubleshooting.description:
If the OpenFeature operator pod is crashing, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n open-feature-operator-system
``` 
Look for any configuration issues or missing dependencies (for example, cert-manager not installed).

troubleshooting.title: Helm installation fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm installation fails, confirm cert-manager is Ready and check for existing resources that may conflict:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
kubectl get all -n open-feature-operator-system
``` 
Ensure there are no existing deployments or services with the same name.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OpenFeature will have the operator running in the 'open-feature-operator-system' namespace with all pods in the Running state, and cert-manager operational as a prerequisite. You can verify this by checking the status of the pods using kubectl.

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster, Helm, kubectl, and cert-manager installed to deploy OpenFeature.
